growth_orientation_rationale: The tax incentive encourages private sector R&D investment, boosting innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The tax allowance was applied starting in 2020.
importance_rationale: The reform provides significant financial incentives for private R&D activities.
description: Denmark temporarily increased the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million, initially for 2020 and 2021, later extended to 2022.
source_quote: The package also included a temporary increase in the R&D enhanced tax allowance to 130% in 2020 and 2021 (later extended to 2022) for R&D expenses up to DKK 850 million.

growth_orientation_rationale: The programme supports green innovation and technology development, contributing to sustainable economic growth.
status_evidence: The programme is actively supporting green R&D initiatives.
importance_rationale: The programme represents a structural change in Denmark's approach to climate and green technology R&D.
description: Denmark's climate programme includes substantial support for green technologies, with DKK 3,900 million allocated for R&D, and DKK 6 billion for the Danish Green Investment Fund. The programme focuses on carbon capture, eco-friendly agriculture, and Power-to-X technologies, aiming to achieve significant emission reductions by 2030.
source_quote: In the longer term, research and development can contribute to provide alternatives to GHG intensive practices and therefore abate emissions with limited leakage effects.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform supports digital innovation and adoption, enhancing productivity and efficiency.
status_evidence: The strategy is planned for launch in 2022.
importance_rationale: The reform introduces a significant new strategy for digitalisation.
description: A digital growth reform package was introduced in 2018, and a new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022 based on the Digitization Partnership recommendations.
source_quote: A digital growth reform package was introduced in 2018 and a new digitalisation strategy will be launched in early 2022.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform enhances access to finance for startups, fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
status_evidence: The plan to expand support measures has been announced.
importance_rationale: The expansion provides significant support for the growth of young innovative firms.
description: Denmark plans to expand existing support measures to provide more equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase for young firms.
source_quote: Expand existing support measures to provide more equity finance and access to capital in the scale-up phase.

growth_orientation_rationale: The project supports renewable energy infrastructure, contributing to sustainable growth and energy security.
status_evidence: The project has been announced with detailed plans.
importance_rationale: The development of wind energy islands is a major infrastructure project with significant economic and environmental impact.
description: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands to provide renewable electricity, sustainable fuels, and interconnection with other countries, with the first stage of the North Sea project estimated at EUR 29 billion.
source_quote: The Danish government is planning to develop two wind energy islands, an artificial island in the North Sea and the Baltic island of Bornholm.
